pattern: Concatenate both sentences, count each word's global frequency, and return those with a count of exactly one — they appear in exactly one sentence and never repeat.
hints: Split both strings and count how many times each word appears across the combined token list.
A word is only eligible if its total count across both sentences equals exactly one.
Build a frequency map from all tokens at once, then filter entries whose value is 1.

edge_inputs.description: each sentence has one unique word, shared words repeat
a word repeated within one sentence is not uncommon even if absent from the other
no words in common, all are uncommon
every word appears in both sentences, so result is empty
single-word sentences, same word, result is empty
word appears once in s1 but also once in s2, making it non-uncommon
s2 has multiple unique words while s1 contributes none
one sentence is a single repeated word, other has a mix

stress.description: random sentences built from a small alphabet to produce many repeats and varied frequency profiles
stress.note: small vocabulary ensures many repeat collisions and edge frequency counts
